title: Jam drops
subtitle: Buttery thumbprint biscuits with jam centres
excerpt: Twenty-four shortbread-style biscuits, each with a thumbprint filled with raspberry jam. Beginner-friendly, crowd-pleasing, and ready in under 30 minutes. The biscuit base is a simple creamed butter and sugar dough.
sourceNotes: Thumbprint biscuits with jam centres are documented in British, American, and Scandinavian household baking from the late nineteenth century. Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861, Project Gutenberg #10136), gives a comparable buttery biscuit with jam applied before baking. Fannie Farmer, The Boston Cooking-School Cook Book (1896, Project Gutenberg), includes a filled biscuit of the same type. Cross-referenced with pre-1928 American and British domestic baking guides.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keep in an airtight tin for up to 5 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven. Conventional 180°C. The biscuits should be pale gold; overbaking makes them dry.

glossaryTerms.definition: The indentation in a thumbprint biscuit is pressed before baking, and the jam is added before or after baking depending on the recipe. Adding jam before baking (as here) sets it into the biscuit and gives a jammy, slightly caramelised centre; adding it after baking keeps the jam fresher and looser. Press the indentation deep enough to hold a generous teaspoon of jam; shallow wells cause the jam to run over the edge. If the dough cracks around the indent, it is too dry: add a teaspoon of milk.

content.text: Jam drops are shortbread-based thumbprint biscuits: a buttery dough rolled into balls, pressed with a thumb to make a well, and filled with jam before baking. The
goes in before baking so the jam sets into the biscuit. Twenty-four biscuits, ready in 30 minutes.

Heat the oven to 170°C fan (180°C conventional). Line two baking trays with paper. Beat the butter and sugar together until pale and creamy, about 2 minutes. Add the egg yolks and vanilla and beat until combined. Sift in the flour and salt and mix to a smooth, non-sticky dough.

Roll the dough into 24 equal balls and place on the lined trays, spaced 4 cm apart. Press a deep indentation into each ball with your thumb or the end of a wooden spoon. Fill each well with about half a teaspoon of jam; do not overfill or it will overflow. Bake for 13 to 15 minutes, until the biscuits are pale gold at the edges. Cool on the trays for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.
